channel.title: Data Breach & Ransomware Aggregator
channel.description: Real-time data breach notifications from state registries, federal sources, and ransomware trackers

item.title: Large-scale Roblox hacking operation shut down by Ukrainian authorities
item.description:
Ukrainian police arrested three hackers who hijacked 610,000 Roblox accounts and sold them for $225,000 in profit. Police in Ukraine arrested three suspects accused of hacking over 610,000 Roblox accounts and selling them for about $225,000. Officers carried out multiple searches in Lviv, seizing cash, phones, computers, laptops, tablets, and USB drives. The operation disrupted […]
📰 Source: Security Affairs | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: AI Phishing Is No. 1 With a Bullet for Cyberattackers
item.description:
In the past six months, companies have seen a significant influx of AI-powered phishing, as cyberattackers progress from small campaigns to 1-to-1 personalized attacks.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Parsing Agentic Offensive Security's Existential Threat
item.description:
Some fear frontier LLMs like Claude Mythos and OpenAI's GPT-5.5 will lead to cybersecurity annihilation. Ari Herbert-Voss notes this could be an opportunity.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: 20-Year-Old Malware Rewrites History of Cyber Sabotage
item.description:
Researchers have uncovered a malware framework dubbed "fast16" that predates Stuxnet by five years.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: UNC6692 Combines Social Engineering, Malware, Cloud Abuse
item.description:
A newly discovered threat actor is using Microsoft Teams, AWS S3 buckets, and custom "Snow" malware in a multipronged campaign.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Feuding Ransomware Groups Leak Each Other's Data
item.description:
When 0APT and KryBit attacked each other, they exposed infrastructure and operational data, giving defenders rare insight into ransomware operations.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: BlueNoroff Uses Fake Zoom Calls to Turn Victims Into Attack Lures
item.description:
The North Korean group is using stolen victim videos, AI-generated avatars, and fake Zoom calls to scale malware attacks against cryptocurrency executives.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Lotus Wiper Attack Targets Venezuelan Energy Firms, Utilities
item.description:
An analysis of the destructive malware reveals sophisticated living-off-the-land (LotL) techniques and detailed strategies for the widespread deletion of data.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Vect 2.0 Ransomware Acts as Wiper, Thanks to Design Error
item.description:
The emerging ransomware has been deployed against victims of the TeamPCP supply chain attacks, but organizations should think twice before paying for a decryptor.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: AI Finds 38 Security Flaws in Electronic Health Record Platform
item.description:
Flaws in OpenEMR's platform — used by more than 100,000 healthcare providers — enabled database compromise, remote code execution, and data theft.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Vimeo Confirms User and Customer Data Breach
item.description:
The ShinyHunters group is threatening to leak stolen files unless Vimeo agrees to pay a ransom. The post Vimeo Confirms User and Customer Data Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Cyber Insurance Data Gives CISOs New Ammo for Budget Talks
item.description:
Boards may ignore alerts, but they listen to losses: new data from Resilience links security gaps directly to financial impact. The post Cyber Insurance Data Gives CISOs New Ammo for Budget Talks appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Critical GitHub Vulnerability Exposed Millions of Repositories
item.description:
The remote code execution flaw CVE-2026-3854 was found to impact GitHub.com and GitHub Enterprise Server. The post Critical GitHub Vulnerability Exposed Millions of Repositories appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Chrome 147, Firefox 150 Security Updates Rolling Out
item.description:
The browser refreshes resolve critical and high-severity vulnerabilities that could lead to arbitrary code execution. The post Chrome 147, Firefox 150 Security Updates Rolling Out appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Iranian Cyber Group Handala Targets US Troops in Bahrain
item.description:
US service members received WhatsApp messages claiming they would be targeted with drones and missiles. The post Iranian Cyber Group Handala Targets US Troops in Bahrain appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Checkmarx Confirms Data Stolen in Supply Chain Attack
item.description:
The hackers exfiltrated the data from Checkmarx’s GitHub environment on March 30, a week after publishing malicious code. The post Checkmarx Confirms Data Stolen in Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Fresh LiteLLM Vulnerability Exploited Shortly After Disclosure
item.description:
The vulnerability allows attackers to read data from a LiteLLM proxy’s database and potentially modify it. The post Fresh LiteLLM Vulnerability Exploited Shortly After Disclosure appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Cyber Command, NSA chief warns foreign adversaries likely to target midterms
item.description:
Army Gen. Joshua Rudd told lawmakers “we are postured and ready to support as required or tasked, making sure that we safeguard our elections.”
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: European Commission accuses Meta of breaching child safety rules
item.description:
The platforms allegedly flouted the bloc’s Digital Services Act (DSA) by “failing to diligently identify, assess and mitigate the risks of minors under 13 years old accessing their services,” the commission said.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
item.description:
AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and n
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
item.description:
A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
item.description:
The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
item.description:
A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
item.description:
An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
item.description:
Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: HIPAA Violation Fines
item.description:
HIPAA violation fines can be issued by the Department of Health and Human Services’ Office for Civil Rights (OCR) and […] The post HIPAA Violation Fines appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Former FBI Deputy Cyber Chief Calls for Terrorism Classification for Healthcare Ransomware Actors
item.description:
At a recent joint hearing by the Subcommittee on Border Security and Enforcement and the Subcommittee on Cybersecurity and Infrastructure […] The post Former FBI Deputy Cyber Chief Calls for Terrorism Classification for Healthcare Ransomware Actors appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: South Texas Oncology and Hematology Pays $1.1M to Settle Data Breach Lawsuit
item.description:
South Texas Oncology and Hematology, a San Antonio, TX-based provider of leading-edge cancer treatment and other medical services, has settled […] The post South Texas Oncology and Hematology Pays $1.1M to Settle Data Breach Lawsuit appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Cyberattacks Announced by Florida Physician Specialists & Mile Bluff Medical Center
item.description:
Florida Physician Specialists has started notifying patients affected by a November 2025 hacking incident. Mile Bluff Medical Center in Wisconsin […] The post Cyberattacks Announced by Florida Physician Specialists & Mile Bluff Medical Center appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: FREE Webinar: 2025 HIPAA Breaches & Fines: What Went Wrong and Your 2026 Action Plan
item.description:
In 2025, hundreds of healthcare data breaches exposed tens of millions of patient records — and the OCR enforcement record […] The post FREE Webinar: 2025 HIPAA Breaches & Fines: What Went Wrong and Your 2026 Action Plan appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Medical Device Maker Medtronic Announces Data Breach
item.description:
The medical device manufacturing giant Medtronic has confirmed that hackers breached its network and exfiltrated data. The company announced the […] The post Medical Device Maker Medtronic Announces Data Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Settlements Agreed to Resolve Two Class Action Healthcare Data Breach Lawsuits
item.description:
Settlements have received preliminary approval from the courts to resolve class action data breach lawsuits against Dove Healthcare Management Services […] The post Settlements Agreed to Resolve Two Class Action Healthcare Data Breach Lawsuits appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.description:

🎭 Threat Actor: QILIN | 📰 Source: Red Packet Security | 🏷️ Type: Ransomware

item.title: Regulator fines Fidelity Brokerage Services $1.25M over data breach
item.description:
Melanie Waddell reports: William Galvin, Massachusetts’ top securities regulator, ordered Fidelity Brokerage Services on Monday to pay $1.25 million for failing to enforce appropriate cybersecurity controls that resulted in a data breach affecting about 77,000 customers. “After learning of the breach, Fidelity also failed to notify many impacted residents, including the relatives and minor children... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Cherry Health continues to experience issues, but hasn’t publicly acknowledged ransomware attack (Updated)
item.description:
Michael Martin reports: Cherry Health says it is dealing with ongoing technology issues, but days into the disruption, officials have not explained what’s causing them. In a notice posted to their website, the health system said it is “experiencing technology issues across Cherry Health, including our phone system.” Their clinics remain open for scheduled visits.... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Alleged member of Scattered Spider arrested in Finland, U.S. seeks extradition
item.description:
Jason Meisner reports: The high-tech cyber hacker who goes by the online moniker “Bouquet” jetted around the world, from Dubai to Thailand to New York, staying in five-star hotels and flashing cash and jewels, federal authorities allege. As his internet attacks grew bolder and more sophisticated, he taunted the FBI for being a step behind,... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: The “BlueLeaks 2.0” Breach: Will there be any accountability? Senators start with transparency.
item.description:
A DataBreaches.net Editorial The “BlueLeaks 2.0” data breach may be the worst privacy and data security breach affecting students that DataBreaches has seen in 20 years of reporting on breaches affecting the education sector. If people thought the Power School incident was the worst ever, hold my coffee. Who will hold P3 Global Intel (“P3”)... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: In Moldova, hackers attacked a medical database, damaging 30% of the information
item.description:
UNN reports: Moldova’s Cybersecurity Agency has reported a large-scale attack on the country’s main medical database, resulting in damage to around 30% of the information, according to Point, as reported by UNN. The agency’s deputy director said the attackers had been targeting the platform over the past month. The database is a key hub collecting data... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: AR: Pine Bluff School District loses $3.2 million in business email compromise attack
item.description:
THV11 News reports: Pine Bluff School District Superintendent Dr. Jennifer Barbaree broke her silence Monday evening after a cyberattack that cost the district millions. According to district officials, the incident happened on December 17. In a statement, and now confirmed during a board meeting, officials say a wire transfer of more than $3.2 million was... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Cyberattack targeting Asian Football Confederation involves personal info of high-profile athletes like Ronaldo
item.description:
Nogo Mania reports: The football world faces a serious security crisis. A large-scale cyberattack targeted the Asian Football Confederation, exposing sensitive data linked to more than 150,000 players and staff. The breach ranks among the most serious incidents in football history. Reports state that the leaked information includes passport copies, contracts, email addresses, and personal identification data. The... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Over 200 Japanese firms have paid ransomware attackers; 60% fail to recover data
item.description:
Data from Japanese firms indicates that paying ransom is unlikely to enable full recovery of encrypted data. Japan Today reports: At least 222 Japanese companies have paid ransomware attackers in the past, yet about 60 percent of them still failed to recover their data, according to a recent survey. Of 1,107 firms that responded to... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Almost one year after discovery, Sandhills Medical Foundation notifies 169,017 people affected by a cyberattack
item.description:
On April 28, Sandhills Medical Foundation in South Carolina notified the Maine Attorney General’s Office of a data breach that affected a total of 169,017 people, only 8 of whom are Maine residents. Their notification to the state and those affected comes almost a year to the day since they first experienced the breach. According... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: VECT Ransomware is a Wiper, Not Ransomware — Don’t Bother Paying, Says Check Point Research
item.description:
Check Point Researchers recently dug into all three versions of VECT’s ransomware. And what they found should concern anyone who discovers they have been locked by it. From their blog post: Ransomware is supposed to be reversible. The attacker locks your files, holds the key, and returns it when you pay. That’s the business model. VECT’s software... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Checkmarx confirms LAPSUS$ hackers leaked its stolen GitHub data
item.description:
Application security company Checkmarx has confirmed that the LAPSUS$ threat group leaked data stolen from its private GitHub repository. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: US reportedly charges Scattered Spider hacker arrested in Finland
item.description:
A 19-year-old dual United States and Estonian citizen arrested in Finland earlier this month faces federal charges in the U.S. alleging he was a prolific member of the notorious Scattered Spider hacking collective. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Video service Vimeo confirms Anodot breach exposed user data
item.description:
Vimeo has disclosed that data belonging to some of its customers and users has been accessed without authorization following the recent breach at the Anodot data anomaly detection company. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Hackers are exploiting a critical LiteLLM pre-auth SQLi flaw
item.description:
Hackers are targeting sensitive information stored in the LiteLLM open-source large-language model (LLM) gateway by exploiting a critical vulnerability tracked as CVE-2026-42208. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Broken VECT 2.0 ransomware acts as a data wiper for large files
item.description:
Researchers are warning that the VECT 2.0 ransomware has a problem in the way it handles encryption nonces that leads to permanently destroying larger files rather than encrypt them. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Learning from the Vercel breach: Shadow AI & OAuth sprawl
item.description:
A single third-party OAuth integration can become a direct path into your environment. Push explains how the Vercel breach shows a compromised OAuth app can lead to widespread impact across downstream customers. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Hackers arrested for hijacking and selling 610,000 Roblox accounts
item.description:
The Ukrainian police have arrested three individuals who hacked more than 610,000 Roblox gaming accounts and sold them for a profit of $225,000. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Hackers exploit RCE flaws in Qinglong task scheduler for cryptomining
item.description:
Hackers are exploiting two authentication bypass vulnerabilities in the Qinglong open-source task scheduling tool to deploy cryptominers on developers' servers. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Official SAP npm packages compromised to steal credentials
item.description:
Multiple official SAP npm packages were compromised in what is believed to be a TeamPCP supply-chain attack to steal credentials and authentication tokens from developers' systems. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News
